title: Connector Manifest
description: Declarative manifest for a Netwrix DSPM connector. Lives at the root of a connector source tree as connector.yaml. Single source of truth for the source_types row, Setup form, runtime image binding, and capability gating.

name.description: Globally unique slug. Lowercase, hyphen-separated. Becomes source_types.name.

displayName.description: Shown in the Add Connector picker.

icon.description: Path (relative to manifest) to an SVG/PNG icon.

repository.description: OCI image reference without tag/digest, e.g. ghcr.io/netwrix/connectors/box.

digest.description: Required for signed installs (Phase 3). Optional in prototype.

signing.description: Cosign keyless or key-based signing config. Optional in prototype.

scanTypes.description: Which scan types this connector supports. Drives the buttons rendered for a Source.

operations.description: Verbs the image dispatches at runtime. test_connection is conventionally always present.

additionalProcesses.description: Optional post-processing handlers callable via run-process.

sidecars.description: Framework-managed sidecars to attach to the connector Pod. v1 supports: extraction (Tika + Tesseract for text extraction). When listed, the worker reads EXTRACTION_URL from its env and POSTs file bytes to /v1/extract; see docs/extraction.md.

credentials.description: Credential schema. Drives the Service Account form. Stored as a k8s Secret reference at runtime.

source.description: Per-source configuration schema. Drives the Create Source form.

scan.description: Per-scan-execution config schema. Drives any per-execution overrides.

resources.description: k8s resource requests/limits applied to the connector pod.

extraction.description: Configuration for the extraction sidecar. Only meaningful when capabilities.sidecars includes 'extraction'. Connectors that don't opt into the sidecar may omit this block.

maxDepth.description: How many levels deep the sidecar will recurse into archives. 1 = container only (no archive unwrap). 2 = unwrap one level (typical: ZIP containing PDFs). 3+ = unwrap nested archives (ZIP-of-ZIPs). Default: 2.

type.description: Only 'container' supported in v1. Wasm reserved for future.

egress.description: FQDNs the connector needs to reach. Advisory NetworkPolicy generated from this.

permissions.description: What the connector is allowed to emit. Sidecar enforces.

findingTypes.description: Allowed values for the 'type' field of findings. Three built-ins; custom: prefix for extensions.

auth.description: Authentication methods this connector accepts. Drives the 'Auth Type' picker in the Add Source wizard. Connectors that don't need auth (web crawler, public APIs) can omit the section entirely or declare a single method with type=none.

type.description: Auth model. 'none' for anonymous, 'basic'/'bearer'/'api_key' for inline credentials, 'service_account' to reuse the AA26 SA picker, 'oauth2' for the framework-owned OAuth2 engine (see docs/oauth2-v1.md), 'custom' for connector-defined fields.

displayName.description: Label shown in the Auth Type dropdown / 'Connect with X' button.

description.description: Helper text shown below the dropdown when this method is selected.

fields.description: Fields the user fills in when they pick this method (renders just like spec.source.schema). Standard JSON Schema with x-display, x-secret, etc. Use type=none to skip.

accountTypes.description: When type=service_account, restricts the SA picker to these flavors.

provider.description: OAuth provider short name (e.g. 'dropbox', 'google', 'microsoft', 'salesforce'). Required when type=oauth2. Maps to deployment-level env vars <PROVIDER>_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID / <PROVIDER>_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET and to the callback URL path /api/v1/oauth/callback/<provider>.

iconUrl.description: Optional icon for the 'Connect with X' button. Framework ships defaults for known providers.

authorizationUrl.description: OAuth2 authorize endpoint. May contain {field} placeholders matching entries in urlParams[]. Required when type=oauth2 unless customAuthAdapter is set.

tokenUrl.description: OAuth2 token endpoint. May contain {field} placeholders matching entries in urlParams[]. Required when type=oauth2 unless customAuthAdapter is set.

revocationUrl.description: RFC 7009 token revocation endpoint. Best-effort POST when the source is deleted. Optional.

scopes.description: OAuth scopes requested at authorize time. Required when type=oauth2 unless customAuthAdapter is set.

pkce.description: Whether to use PKCE (RFC 7636). Default true — explicit false only for providers that genuinely don't support PKCE.

extraAuthParams.description: Provider-specific query parameters appended to the authorize URL verbatim. Examples: Dropbox token_access_type=offline, Google access_type=offline + prompt=consent.

extraTokenFields.description: Token-response fields beyond RFC 6749 §5.1 standard set (access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, token_type, scope) that the framework must persist and inject at runtime. Examples: Salesforce instance_url, Slack bot_access_token. Phase 3.

field.description: Placeholder name in authorizationUrl/tokenUrl. {field} in the URL is replaced with the user-supplied value at authorize time.

urlParams.description: Pre-auth user inputs substituted into authorizationUrl/tokenUrl templates. Phase 2 (M365 tenantId, Okta domain).

customAuthAdapter.description: Escape hatch for providers that can't be modeled by the declarative engine. host:port the connector image exposes on its main container. Framework calls /oauth/authorize_url, /oauth/exchange, /oauth/refresh on this address per docs/oauth2-v1.md §6.4. Phase 4. Mutually exclusive with authorizationUrl/tokenUrl/scopes.

methods.description: Available auth methods for this connector. If the array is empty or omitted, the wizard skips the auth step.

scope.description: per-source: each Source in the group gets its own credentials (Databricks PAT, Box token). per-group: one credential bundle covers every Source (AD bind credentials).

sourceTypes.description: One or more source types this connector emits. Each becomes a row in ClickHouse access_analyzer.source_type_registry at upload time and defines an ingestion mapping the runtime sidecar applies to findings tagged with the matching sourceType. See connector-framework/docs/ingestion-mapping-v1.md.

name.description: PascalCase. Globally unique across all installed connectors. The worker tags each finding with this string (envelope.sourceType) so the runtime knows which mapping to apply.

domain.description: Maps to access_analyzer.domain_type_properties.domainName. Drives whether the entity participates in the parentId hierarchy and lands in the principals table.

target.description: v1 supports only the entities table. v2 will allow access_grant and other shapes.

mapping.description: Map from entities columns to a JSON path expression evaluated against each finding event. Only the cross-domain allow-list is permitted; see ingestion-mapping-v1.md §4.1.

additionalProperties.description: Map of namespaced extra-keys to JSON path expressions. Each key MUST match '^<metadata.name>\.[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$' and lands in entities.additionalProperties Map(String, String).

jsonSchemaSubset.description: Embedded JSON Schema used to render forms. Validates as a JSON Schema document.

jsonPathExpr.description: Restricted JSONPath. Root '$' followed by one or more '.<identifier>' segments. No array indexing, filters, or wildcards. Examples: '$.object.url', '$.evidence.raw.etag'.
